0101.title: UNIT MAIL HANDLING ORDER

quotes.text: Unit Mail Handling Order. All Marine Corps commands operating UMRs are responsible for publishing mail handling instructions by current command signature authority. The Unit Mail Handling Order shall include: a. The complete command and personnel mailing addresses. b. Serving MPO location and hours of operation. c. UMR location and hours of operation. d. USPS mail collection box location and hours of last pick up of outgoing mail. e. Mail call time. f. Custom regulations where applicable. g. Command Official Mail Program. h. Security of mail and postal effects. i. Official accountable mail e.g., registered, certified, etc., identification. j. MHPs for personnel temporarily absent from the command e.g., TAD, annual leave, etc. k. Mail delivery during exercises. l. Suspicious package procedures e.g., bombs, anthrax, etc. m. Current directory service application. n. Maintenance of the DFC system. o. Additional postal information deemed appropriate by the promulgating authority. p. Units without UMRs shall provide policy on the incoming and outgoing of official mail.

regulatory.summary: Each Marine Corps command operating a UMR must publish a Unit Mail Handling Order signed by current command signature authority and containing all sixteen required elements (a through p).

actions: Request current Unit Mail Handling Order from the Unit Mail Officer.
howToInspect.callout: No Unit Mail Handling Order published

actions: Confirm coverage of all sixteen lettered elements.
howToInspect.callout: Order missing one or more required elements (a-p)

actions: Confirm copy on file in UMR.
howToInspect.callout: Order signed by prior command authority and not refreshed

questionsToAsk: Who signed the current order?
When was it last updated?
Where is it filed?

0102.title: UNIT MAIL OFFICER AND ASSISTANT APPOINTMENT

quotes.text: Appoint in writing a primary Unit Mail Officer and Assistant Unit Mail Officer(s) to supervise the daily operation of the UMR.
Unit, organization, or agency personnel, assigned to and responsible for performing unit mail handling duties, will be appointed on a Designation Letter signed by the responsible commander.

Units/organizations will maintain a copy of the Designation Letter of Appointment documentation for unit personnel, provide and maintain a current copy at the MPA, and provide one to the individual(s).
Designate in writing a Unit Postal Officer (UPO) or alternate to cover absences of the UPO that exceed more than four days.

regulatory.summary: The Commanding Officer must appoint, in writing, a primary Unit Mail Officer and Assistant Unit Mail Officer(s) on a signed Designation Letter. Copies must be maintained at the unit, provided to the MPA, and given to the appointees.

actions: Request current UMO and Assistant UMO appointment letters.
howToInspect.callout: No Unit Mail Officer or Assistant appointed in writing

actions: Confirm letters signed by current CO.
howToInspect.callout: Appointment letter not current or signed by prior CO

actions: Confirm copy on file at unit.
MPA does not have a copy.

questionsToAsk: Who is the current UMO and Assistant?
When were they appointed?
Where are copies filed?
verification: Current letters on file for primary and assistant.
Copies at MPA and with appointees.

0103.title: MINIMUM TWO MAIL CLERKS OR ORDERLIES PER UNIT/SECTION

quotes.text: Two Unit Mail Clerks/Orderlies shall be appointed at a minimum per each unit/section via DD Form 2260 and DD Form 285.

regulatory.summary: Each unit or section must appoint at a minimum two Unit Mail Clerks or Mail Orderlies, with appointment evidenced by DD Form 2260 and DD Form 285.

actions: Request DD Forms 2260 and 285 for each unit and section.
Identify any section with fewer than two.
howToInspect.callout: Fewer than two Mail Clerks/Orderlies appointed per unit or section

questionsToAsk: How many clerks or orderlies are appointed per section?
Where are DD Forms 285 and 2260 filed?
verification: At least two clerks/orderlies appointed per unit/section.

0104.title: AUTHORIZATION TO RECEIVE AND OPEN OFFICIAL MAIL

quotes.text: Commanding Officers must designate in writing personnel e.g., Unit OMM, etc., authorized to receipt and open official mail, to include official accountable mail addressed to the Commanding Officer. Official mail/official accountable mail must not be opened in the UMR under any circumstance.
Unit Mail Clerks/Orderlies shall not be authorized agents on the designation letter to receipt and open official mail/official accountable mail.

The authorization letter must be signed by the current Commanding Officer. By Direction authority is not authorized. The authorization letter must contain a sample signature of all individuals authorized.

Separate letters may authorize more than one individual for each type of mail. However, any changes to an authorization letter shall void the authority and require a new authorization letter. Authorization letters must be maintained for two years after cancellation in the UMR files.

regulatory.summary: The current Commanding Officer must designate, in writing, personnel authorized to receive and open official and official accountable mail. Letters must be signed by the current CO (no By Direction authority), include sample signatures, exclude Unit Mail Clerks/Orderlies, and be retained two years after cancellation.

howToInspect.callout: No Commanding Officer designation letter for receipt/opening of official mail

actions: Confirm signed by current CO (not By Direction).
Confirm sample signatures of each authorized individual.
howToInspect.callout: Letter signed By Direction or by prior CO

actions: Confirm no Unit Mail Clerks or Orderlies are listed.
howToInspect.callout: Mail Clerks/Orderlies listed as authorized agents (prohibited)

actions: Confirm cancelled letters retained two years.

questionsToAsk: Who is currently authorized to receive and open official mail?
How are changes processed?

0105.title: DESIGNATION DOCUMENTS COMPLETION AND MAINTENANCE

quotes.text: UMRs must file original appointment letter, DD Form 285, or PS Form 3801 and provide a copy to the appointee.
The MPO must retain a copy of Unit Mail Clerk DD Form 285 or PS Form 3801.
Annotate on assigned DD Form 285 the type of mail the appointee is authorized to handle, e.g., OFFICIAL ALL, OFFICIAL EXCEPT ACCOUNTABLE, or PERSONAL EXCEPT ACCOUNTBALE.
Retain appointment, support, and revocation documents on file for two years, per reference (e).

regulatory.summary: Original appointment letters, DD Forms 285, or PS Forms 3801 must be filed in the UMR with copies to the appointee and the MPO. Mail authorization type must be annotated on DD Form 285. Appointment, support, and revocation documents are retained for two years.

actions: Confirm DD Form 285 or PS Form 3801 with mail authorization type annotated.
Confirm copies provided to appointee and MPO.
howToInspect.callout: DD Form 285 mail authorization type not annotated

actions: Confirm revocation documents retained two years.
howToInspect.callout: Revocation documents not retained two years

actions: DD Form 285 mail type not annotated.

questionsToAsk: Where do you file appointment documents?
How do you annotate authorized mail type?
verification: Copies at MPO and with appointee.

0106.title: MAIL HANDLING TRAINING WITHIN REQUIRED PERIOD

quotes.text: Ensure Unit Mail Clerks complete the Mail Clerk Indoctrination Course within 30 days of assuming mail handling duties.
Ensure Mail Orderlies receive unit level training on MHPs prior to assuming mail handling duties.
Unit Mail Officers/Unit Assistant Mail Officers must attend and complete the resident/distant Mail clerk Indoctrination Course within 90 days of appointment.
Attend required mail handling training within 30 days of designation.

regulatory.summary: Unit Mail Officers and Assistants must complete the Mail Clerk Indoctrination Course within 90 days of appointment. Unit Mail Clerks must complete it within 30 days of assuming duties. Mail Orderlies must receive unit-level MHP training prior to assuming duties.

actions: Request Mail Clerk Indoctrination Course certificates for UMO, Assistant, and clerks.
Compute days from appointment to course completion.
Confirm UMO and Assistant completed within 90 days.
Confirm orderlies trained prior to assuming duties.
howToInspect.callout: UMO/Assistant not trained within 90 days of appointment

actions: UMO or Assistant not trained within 90 days.

questionsToAsk: When was each appointee trained?
How is Mail Orderly unit-level training documented?
verification: Mail Orderlies trained prior to assuming duties.

0107.title: UMR STRUCTURAL REQUIREMENTS

quotes.text: Unit Mailroom Structural Requirements. UMRs design/construction shall provide adequate space and security for mail, per references (b), DoD UFC (4-010-01), and DoD UFC (4-020-01). The following are minimum structural requirements for a UMR located in a permanent structure: a. All locks and door hinges must be mounted inside or in such a manner to prevent easy removal. Existing door hinges mounted on the outside of the UMR must be spot welded. b. All accessible windows i.e., windows on the first floor, ground level, reachable via catwalk, or adjoining rooftops shall be equipped with bars/heavy wire mesh to prevent intrusion. c. Walls and ceilings must be constructed to prevent forcible entry. d. Mail delivery receptacles must be installed to prevent access to/from other receptacles or a customer service window.

regulatory.summary: The UMR must meet four minimum structural requirements covering door hinge security, window protection, wall/ceiling integrity, and receptacle isolation.

actions: Confirm hinges mounted inside or spot welded.
Confirm accessible windows have bars or heavy wire mesh.
Confirm walls and ceilings prevent forcible entry.
howToInspect.callout: UMR structural requirements not met (hinges, windows, walls, receptacles)

actions: Accessible window lacks bars or mesh.

questionsToAsk: When was the UMR last inspected for structural compliance?
What corrective work is pending?

0108.title: SPACE AND EQUIPMENT FOR MAIL HANDLING AND SECURITY

quotes.text: Provide adequate space and equipment necessary for proper mail handling/security. Ensure Unit Mail Clerks and Orderlies have sufficient time to perform their duties.
Unit Mailroom Structural Requirements. UMRs design/construction shall provide adequate space and security for mail, per references (b), DoD UFC (4-010-01), and DoD UFC (4-020-01).

regulatory.summary: The Commanding Officer must provide adequate space and equipment for mail handling and security and must ensure clerks and orderlies have sufficient time to perform their duties.

actions: Confirm sufficient workspace for sorting, distribution, and storage.
Confirm essential equipment present (scales, locks, file cabinets, sorting tables, computer).

howToInspect.callout: Mail Clerks/Orderlies lack sufficient time to perform duties

questionsToAsk: What equipment is in the UMR?
Do you have sufficient duty time?

0109.title: RESTRICTED ACCESS TO UMR KEYS

quotes.text: The Unit Mail Officer shall be the custodian of the UMR keys. A list of individuals issued a key must be maintained in a key control log. Strictly control and safeguard the keys at all times. The primary Unit Mail Clerk must annotate the date/time key is received and returned with signature. The key must be return to the Unit Mail Officer daily for proper storage. At no time shall the Unit Mail Clerk be authorized to keep the UMR key overnight.

regulatory.summary: The Unit Mail Officer is the key custodian. A key control log must list every individual issued a key. The primary Unit Mail Clerk must annotate date/time and signature for each receipt and return. Keys must be returned to the UMO daily and never kept overnight by a clerk.

howToInspect.callout: UMO not maintaining custody of UMR keys

actions: Confirm date, time, signature for each receipt and return.
howToInspect.callout: UMR key retained overnight by Mail Clerk (prohibited)

actions: Confirm no overnight retention by clerks.

questionsToAsk: Who is the key custodian?
How is daily return documented?

0110.title: DUPLICATE KEYS AND COMBINATIONS SECURED

quotes.text: The duplicate key(s) or combination to the UMR shall be sealed in a plain envelope or a PS Form 3977, Duplicate Key Envelope (Figure 3-5). A PS Form 3977 can be obtained through the serving MPO. (1) Prior to sealing the duplicate key or combination in the envelope ensure they work properly. The Unit Mail Officer and designated Unit Mail Clerk must seal/sign across the back flap of the envelope. (2) The Unit Mail Officer and designated Unit Mail Clerk shall endorse the front of the envelope to show its contents and date sealed. PS Form 3977 must be kept in a safe that is controlled by the Unit Mail Officer or designated representative.

A new PS Form 3977 shall be prepared when the duplicate key or combination is used. The Unit Mail Officer or designated representative holding the duplicate keys must sign the key log book to indicate the date received and returned.

regulatory.summary: Duplicate keys or combinations must be sealed in a PS Form 3977 (or plain envelope) signed by the UMO and designated Mail Clerk on both seal and front, stored in a UMO-controlled safe. A new envelope is required after each use, with key log entries on receipt and return. Inspector Note: USPS Handbook F-101 and USPS RE-05 are cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify the current PS Form 3977 procedures against those source documents on site, as wording may have updated since policy issuance.

actions: Confirm PS Form 3977 or plain envelope present.
Confirm UMO and Mail Clerk signatures on seal and front.
Confirm contents and date sealed shown.
howToInspect.callout: Duplicate keys/combinations not properly sealed and stored
PS Form 3977 not renewed after each use

actions: Confirm a new PS Form 3977 was prepared after each use.
Confirm log entries on receipt and return.
Old envelope still in use after withdrawal.

questionsToAsk: Where is the duplicate key envelope stored?
When was it last opened?

0111.title: DAILY KEY RETURN AND LOG ANNOTATION

quotes.text: The primary Unit Mail Clerk must annotate the date/time key is received and returned with signature. The key must be return to the Unit Mail Officer daily for proper storage. At no time shall the Unit Mail Clerk be authorized to keep the UMR key overnight.

regulatory.summary: The Unit Mail Clerk must return the UMR key to the UMO daily. Each receipt and return must be annotated in the key control log with date, time, and signature. Overnight retention by a clerk is prohibited. Inspector Note: USPS ASM par 273.434 is cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify the corresponding USPS ASM section on site since USPS publications update independently.

actions: Confirm each entry shows date, time, signature on receipt and return.
Confirm returns occur each operating day.
howToInspect.callout: Overnight retention by clerk

questionsToAsk: At what time do you return the key each day?
Where is the key stored overnight?
verification: Log entries complete with date, time, signature.

0112.title: SEPARATE KEY CONTROL LOG FOR CLERK-TO-CLERK TRANSFER

quotes.text: Maintain a separate key control log to transfer the key to another designated Unit Mail Clerk. This key control log must be kept in the UMR.

regulatory.summary: The UMR must maintain a separate key control log for transferring the key from one designated Mail Clerk to another. This log must be kept in the UMR.

actions: Confirm a separate log exists for clerk-to-clerk key transfers.
howToInspect.callout: No separate clerk-to-clerk transfer log maintained

actions: Confirm log is kept in the UMR.

questionsToAsk: Where is the clerk-to-clerk transfer log kept?

0113.title: DD FORM 1115 DISPLAYED

quotes.text: All UMRs must prominently display DD Form 1115, Mailroom No Admittance Except to Authorized Personnel, on or near the entrance. DD Form 1115 shall provide an example of a personal/command mailing address and hours of operation (Figure 3-4).

regulatory.summary: The UMR must prominently display DD Form 1115 on or near the entrance, with example personal/command mailing address and hours of operation.

actions: Confirm DD Form 1115 posted prominently on or near the entrance.
howToInspect.callout: DD Form 1115 not displayed at UMR entrance

questionsToAsk: Where is DD Form 1115 posted?

0114.title: ENTRY LIMITED TO AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL

quotes.text: Ensure entry to the UMR is limited to authorized personnel.

Limit access to the UMR to designated postal personnel conducting official business e.g., inspections, site visits, etc. Allow maintenance personnel and work details access only when escorted or under constant surveillance by a Unit Mail Clerk. The Unit Mail Clerk must verify identification and authorization of all personnel prior to allowing entry into the UMR.

regulatory.summary: UMR entry is limited to authorized postal personnel on official business. Maintenance personnel and work details may enter only when escorted or under constant surveillance. Mail Clerks must verify identification and authorization prior to allowing entry.

howToInspect.step: Interview The Clerk On Entry Procedures
actions: Confirm clerk verifies ID and authorization before entry.
howToInspect.callout: Unauthorized personnel allowed entry to UMR

actions: Confirm escort or constant surveillance during entry by non-postal personnel.
howToInspect.callout: Maintenance personnel unescorted in UMR

questionsToAsk: How do you verify entry authorization?
How do you handle maintenance personnel access?
verification: ID and authorization verified prior to entry.

0115.title: UMR FREE OF ITEMS UNRELATED TO MAIL

quotes.text: Only U.S. mail, postal records, and essential furniture shall be in the UMR. Personal briefcases, backpacks, paper sacks, or other containers are not authorized in the UMR. The UMR must not be used to store gear that is not related to the delivery or processing of U.S. mail.

Items sent via guard mail and the direct delivery of Federal Express, United Parcel Service, or other private carriers to business addresses must not be processed/stored in the UMR. These types of items are not U.S. mail.

regulatory.summary: Only U.S. mail, postal records, and essential furniture are authorized in the UMR. Personal containers, non-mail gear, and items delivered by private carriers must not be present.

howToInspect.callout: UMR contains items unrelated to U.S. mail or postal records

actions: Confirm each item is U.S. mail, postal record, or essential furniture.
Identify personal containers or non-mail items.
howToInspect.callout: Private carrier (FedEx, UPS) items stored in UMR (prohibited)

questionsToAsk: What is in the UMR that is not U.S. mail or furniture?

0116.title: WEEKLY UNANNOUNCED UMR INSPECTIONS

quotes.text: The current IGMC FA Checklist, Postal Affairs (UMR) must be used to conduct the inspection requirement. The inspection checklist can be downloaded at the IGMC website. Inspections must not be routine to establish a trend.

Unit Mail Officers or Assistant Unit Mail Officers shall supervise the UMR daily and ensure the mail is handled correctly/promptly. Random and unannounced weekly inspections shall be conducted as required.

Weekly UMR inspection checklists may be destroyed after the unit has been inspected by a Marine Corps Postal Inspector. The quarterly UMR inspection checklist shall be kept on file in the UMR for two years.

regulatory.summary: The UMO or Assistant UMO must conduct random, unannounced weekly UMR inspections using the current IGMC FA Checklist, Postal Affairs. Weekly checklists may be destroyed after Postal Inspector inspection. Quarterly UMR checklists are retained in the UMR for two years.

actions: Request weekly inspection records for the prior quarter.
howToInspect.callout: Random weekly UMR inspections not conducted

actions: Confirm the most recent IGMC checklist version was used.
Confirm inspections not routine in timing.
howToInspect.callout: Quarterly UMR inspection checklists not retained two years

actions: Confirm quarterly checklists retained two years.

questionsToAsk: Which IG checklist version do you use?
How do you randomize inspection timing?

0117.title: SUSTAINED COMPUTER ACCESS FOR DIRECTORY MAIL

quotes.text: Provide sustained and networked computer access in the UMR to process directory mail via the Directory Service Program.

regulatory.summary: The Commanding Officer must provide sustained and networked computer access in the UMR for processing directory mail via the Directory Service Program.

howToInspect.callout: No sustained networked computer access in UMR

questionsToAsk: When did you last process directory mail electronically?

0118.title: CURRENT PUBLICATIONS AND DIRECTIVES

quotes.text: Publications. Each UMR is required to maintain copies of the listed current postal publications/directives. Electronic copies meet this requirement. a. DoD Postal Manual. b. MCO 5110.4B, Marine Corps Postal Affairs and Official Mail Program. c. Installation/Base Order for Postal Affairs. d. Unit Mail Handling Order.

regulatory.summary: Each UMR must maintain current copies of: DoD Postal Manual, MCO 5110.4B, Installation/Base Order for Postal Affairs, and Unit Mail Handling Order. Electronic copies satisfy this requirement.

actions: Confirm Unit Mail Handling Order present.
howToInspect.callout: Required publication(s) missing from UMR

actions: Confirm each is the current edition.

questionsToAsk: Where do you access each required publication?
verification: All four publications present (paper or electronic).

0119.title: DD FORM 285 IN POSSESSION

quotes.text: Physically retain one copy of DD Form 285 at all times when performing mail handling duties.
Physically retain one copy of DD Form 285 when performing mail handling duties and safeguard the mail at all times.

Unit Mail Clerks/Orderlies must present their issued DD Form 285, DoD Common Access Card (CAC), or appropriate identification when performing mail handling duties. Appointment letter and identification shall be validated prior to the delivery of mail.

regulatory.summary: Mail Clerks and Orderlies must physically retain one copy of DD Form 285 at all times when performing mail handling duties and must present it (or CAC/appropriate ID) when receipting for mail.

actions: Ask each on duty to produce DD Form 285.
howToInspect.callout: Mail Clerk/Orderly not in possession of DD Form 285

questionsToAsk: May I see your DD Form 285?

0120.title: PROTECTION OF MAIL TO/FROM MPA

quotes.text: Use a closed-body government vehicle equipped with lockable doors to transport mail to and from the serving MPO. When other types of government vehicles are used, Unit Mail Clerks/Orderlies shall ride in the mail hold compartment and maintain visual contact with the mail. Always protect the mail from inclement weather.

Privately owned vehicles (POVs) shall not be used to transport unit mail. Commanders can request a written temporary POV authorization to the MCPO/MPO Supervisor. The use of POVs to transport mail must not be a routine or permanent standing operating procedure. It is the command's responsibility to coordinate and obtain a government vehicle to meet the mail transportation requirement.
Passengers not authorized to handle mail must not have access to the mail during vehicle transport.
USPS equipment e.g., letter trays, flat bins, etc., shall only be used to transport mail. USPS equipment shall be returned to the serving MPO when not in use.

regulatory.summary: Mail to/from the MPA must move in a closed-body government vehicle with lockable doors, with the clerk or orderly maintaining visual contact and protecting mail from weather. POVs are not authorized except by written temporary authorization. Unauthorized passengers must not access the mail. USPS equipment is used only for mail transport.

howToInspect.callout: POV used to transport mail without written authorization

actions: If POV used, pull written temporary authorization.
howToInspect.callout: Unauthorized passengers had access to mail in transit

actions: Confirm USPS equipment used only for mail.

questionsToAsk: What vehicle is used to transport mail?
How do you protect mail in transit?
verification: USPS equipment used only for mail.

0121.title: PRIVILEGED NATURE OF MAIL AND POSTAL RECORDS

quotes.text: The privacy of mail and mail records must not be violated. Unit Mail Clerks/Orderlies shall not break the seal of any mail matter. Information about mail or mail records must not be released without authorization. Requests for information shall be referred to the Unit Mail Officer.
Personal addresses are privileged information and shall not be divulged to anyone except in the course of official business.

regulatory.summary: Mail Clerks and Orderlies must not break mail seals or release information about mail or mail records without authorization. Personal addresses are privileged. Requests for information must be referred to the UMO.

actions: Ask about handling of information requests.
howToInspect.callout: Mail Clerk released information about mail without authorization

actions: Confirm clerks know to refer requests to UMO.
Confirm clerks know not to break seals.

questionsToAsk: What do you do if asked for a service member's mailing address?
What if you find a mail piece unsealed?
verification: Clerks refer information requests to UMO.

0122.title: MAIL DATE STAMP ON REVERSE SIDE

quotes.text: Back stamp all mail received in the UMR to show date of receipt.

Unit Mail Clerks are required to date stamp the reverse side of the mail articles received. The UMR must return daily or the next business day all rework/redirect mail to the serving MPO for proper disposition. Mail shall not be held for more than 24 hours, not including weekends and holidays, without proper supporting documentation.

regulatory.summary: Mail received in the UMR must be date stamped on the reverse side to show day of receipt. Rework or redirect mail must be returned to the MPO the next business day, with mail not held over 24 hours absent supporting documentation.

actions: Pull a sample of mail received.
howToInspect.callout: Mail not date-stamped on reverse side

actions: Stamp on front instead of reverse.

questionsToAsk: When do you back stamp incoming mail?

0123.title: MAIL ORDERLIES PICK UP MAIL AT DESIGNATED TIMES

quotes.text: Pick up mail daily and ensure personal mail is delivered only to the addressee. Subsequent delivery to addressee's Officer in Charge (OIC)/Non-commissioned Officer in Charge (NCOIC) is not authorized.

The UMR must maintain a daily mail delivery receipt log for the Mail Orderlies. The mail delivery receipt log annotations must match assigned section, DD Form 285, and DD Form 2260. The mail receipt log sheet shall be kept for 1 year and then destroyed.

Mail Orderlies must pick up mail at the designated time daily. Mail Orderlies shall return all undeliverable mail to the UMR daily. Supporting documentation e.g., TAD, annual leave, etc., shall be provided stating the reason for mail non-delivery. Mail Orderlies must not make endorsements on the mail.

regulatory.summary: Mail Orderlies must pick up mail at designated times daily, deliver personal mail only to the addressee, return undeliverable mail to the UMR each day with supporting documentation, and not endorse mail. The UMR maintains a daily delivery receipt log retained one year.

actions: Confirm pickup occurs at designated times.
Confirm undeliverable mail returned same day with documentation.
howToInspect.callout: Orderly endorsed mail (prohibited)

actions: Delivery to NCOIC instead of addressee.

questionsToAsk: What times do you pick up mail?
What do you do with undeliverable mail?
verification: Undeliverable mail returned same day with documentation.

0124.title: TIMELY MOBILE UNIT MAIL ROUTING INFORMATION

quotes.text: Unit Mail Officers shall submit a mail routing request (Figure 3-14) to the serving MPO 30 days prior to a unit deployment.
The MPO must provide a deployed mailing address and MHP no earlier than 20 days from the estimated date of departure.
Under no circumstance shall a unit utilized another unit's deploying or sister service address in order to circumvent the 20 day policy. Unauthorized actions cause mail delays and returns.
The Unit Mail Officer is responsible to distribute the MHP to supporting units.

regulatory.summary: UMOs must submit a mail routing request to the serving MPO 30 days prior to deployment. The MPO provides the deployed address and MHP no earlier than 20 days before departure. UMOs must distribute the MHP to supporting units. Using another unit's deploying address is prohibited.

actions: Request list of unit deployments in the last 12 months.
Confirm routing request submitted at least 30 days prior.
howToInspect.callout: Mail routing request not submitted 30 days prior to deployment

actions: Confirm MHP distributed to supporting units.

questionsToAsk: When did you submit the most recent routing request?
How do you distribute the MHP?
verification: Routing requests submitted 30 days prior to deployment.
No use of another unit's deploying address.

0125.title: ABSENTEE BALLOTING MATERIAL HANDLING

quotes.text: Absentee Balloting Materials. All voting materials must be processed per the current voting action/support plan. The processing of absentee ballots shall be given special attention and must be handled in the most expeditious manner. UMRs must provide immediate directory service to voting and balloting material.

regulatory.summary: All voting and balloting material must be processed per the current voting action/support plan, handled with special attention and expedited, and provided immediate directory service. Inspector Note: MPM par 19.2.a(1) is cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify the corresponding MPM paragraph on site as MPM revisions occur on a defined update cycle.

howToInspect.step: Interview Mail Clerks On Ballot Handling
actions: Ask how ballot material is identified and prioritized.
Confirm plan is current and on file.
howToInspect.callout: Ballot material not given expedited handling

questionsToAsk: What do you do with absentee ballot material?
Where is the voting plan kept?

0126.title: REFUSED MAIL HANDLING

quotes.text: Mail Refused. Addressees may refuse mail that they do not want to receive. The addressee must endorse the front of the envelope or wrapper, Refused, with signature and date. If the addressee declines to make the endorsement, the Unit Mail Clerk/Orderly shall endorse the mail Refused by addressee, sign, date, and return it to the serving MPO.

regulatory.summary: When an addressee refuses mail, the addressee must endorse the front Refused with signature and date. If the addressee declines, the clerk or orderly endorses Refused by addressee with signature and date and returns it to the serving MPO.

actions: Confirm understanding of both addressee endorsement and clerk endorsement options.
howToInspect.callout: Refused mail not properly endorsed before return

actions: Clerk fails to return to MPO.

questionsToAsk: What do you do when an addressee refuses mail?
verification: Proper endorsements present on returned mail.

0127.title: OPEN BY MISTAKE HANDLING

quotes.text: Mail Open by Mistake. Mail opened by mistake must be re-sealed, endorsed Opened by Mistake, dated, and signed by the person who opened the mail piece. Mail delivery shall be corrected or returned to the serving MPO.

regulatory.summary: Mail opened by mistake must be re-sealed and endorsed Opened by Mistake with date and signature of the person who opened it. Delivery is then corrected or the piece returned to the MPO.

actions: Ask procedure for mail opened by mistake.
howToInspect.callout: Open-by-mistake endorsement not applied

actions: Confirm clerks know to reseal, endorse, sign, and date.

questionsToAsk: What do you do if you open mail by mistake?
verification: Clerks understand re-seal and endorsement procedure.

0128.title: MAIL RECEIVED OPEN, DAMAGED, OR MISSING CONTENTS

quotes.text: Unit Mail Clerks must ensure any single/close bag damaged articles are properly rewrapped. Every effort shall be made to match articles found loose with their original envelopes or wrappers. Return unmatched articles to the serving MPO.
Unit Mail Clerks must endorse damaged articles, Received in damaged condition. Annotate date of receipt, clerk's initials, and identify the unit repairing the article.

regulatory.summary: Damaged articles must be rewrapped and endorsed Received in damaged condition with date of receipt, clerk's initials, and identifying unit. Loose articles are matched to their wrappers when possible; unmatched articles are returned to the MPO.

actions: Ask procedure for damaged or open mail.
howToInspect.callout: Damaged article not endorsed Received in damaged condition

actions: Confirm Received in damaged condition endorsement, with date, initials, and repair unit.
Unmatched articles not returned to MPO.

questionsToAsk: What do you do with damaged mail or loose contents?
verification: Endorsements include date, initials, and repair unit.

0129.title: PS FORM 3849 DELIVERY INSTRUCTIONS FOR PERSONAL ACCOUNTABLE MAIL

quotes.text: Addressees are responsible for picking up their own accountable mail. Addressees can authorize a responsible agent to pick up their personal accountable mail. Authorization is provided on reverse side of PS Form 3849 with annotated agent name and signature.

0131.title: MAIL BOMB AND SUSPICIOUS MAIL DETECTION

quotes.text: Immediately notify the MCPO/MPO Supervisor of any suspicious items. If the MCPO is unavailable contact the first responders.

Mail Bombs. Mail bombs may exhibit the following unique characteristics that can assist personnel in identifying a suspected letter or parcel: a. Have restricted endorsements such as Personal or Private. b. Have an inaccurate address, name, or title. c. Have distorted handwriting, homemade labels, or cut-and-paste lettering for the name and address. d. Have visible protruding wires, aluminum foil, oil stains, or emit a peculiar odor. e. Have an excessive amount of postage stamps affixed. f. Letters may have a rigid feel or an uneven/lopsided appearance. g. Parcels may have unprofessional wrapping with several combinations of tape used to secure the package. Items may be endorsed Fragile-Handle with Care or Rush-Do-Not-Delay. h. Parcels may have a buzzing, ticking, or sloshing noise.

Suspicious Mail. Suspicious mailings that are unable to be verified by the addressee/sender are handled as follows: a. Do not open the article. b. Isolate the article and evacuate the area. c. Do not put the article in water or a confined space i.e., desk drawer, filing cabinet, etc. d. Open windows in the immediate area to assist in venting potentially explosive gases. e. Do not take any chances or worry about possible embarrassment if the item turns out to be a false alarm. Contact the local military law enforcement agency if there is any reason to believe a letter or parcel is dangerous.

regulatory.summary: Mail clerks must recognize mail bomb indicators (per Ch. 6 par 2 a-h) and follow suspicious mail procedures (do not open, isolate, evacuate, do not place in water or confined space, open windows, contact law enforcement). The MCPO/MPO Supervisor must be notified immediately or first responders if unavailable. Inspector Note: MPM par 7.2.b and 8.2.j are cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify those paragraphs on site as MPM updates separately from MCO 5110.4B.

actions: Ask the mail bomb indicators they look for.
Ask the suspicious mail response procedure.
Confirm Poster 84 displayed in mail handling locations.
howToInspect.callout: Annual mail bomb/suspicious mail training not conducted

actions: Confirm annual mail bomb/suspicious security training coordinated with law enforcement and documented.

0135.title: CHAIN OF RECEIPTS FOR OFFICIAL ACCOUNTABLE MAIL

quotes.text: A chain of delivery receipts shall cover all accountable mail. The Unit Mail Clerk must be able to account for all accountable mail. Accountability shall be provided by physical possession of article, or by showing delivery of article to authorized agent.

The serving post office shall prepare a PS Form 3883, Firm Delivery Receipt for Accountable and Bulk Delivery Mail (Figure 3-8). The MPO or serving post office must retain the original copy. The Unit Mail Clerk shall receive a copy with the mail piece. The Unit Mail Clerk shall ensure the accountable mail pieces are listed on PS Form 3883 and are in good delivery condition. PS Forms 3883 must be retained in the UMR for two years.

The Unit Mail Clerk shall prepare for delivery accountable mail items on a new PS Form 3883 or logbook that similarly captures the delivery details of the accountable mail e.g., registered mail, article number, etc. The Unit Mail Clerk must annotate date delivered, delivered by, authorized agent's printed name, and signature.

Official accountable mail stored in the UMR overnight must have an approved security container that meets the SECRET requirements, per reference (p). UMRs that do not meet the requirement must return all undelivered official accountable mail on PS Form 3883 daily to the serving MPO.

The Unit Mail Officer must initial daily each PS Form 3883 for effective delivery verification. A transfer of accountable mail to subsequent Unit Mail Clerk must require delivery on PS Form 3883. A new PS Form 3883 shall be prepared to make effective delivery to an authorized agent.

regulatory.summary: All accountable mail must be covered by a chain of delivery receipts using PS Form 3883, with date delivered, delivered by, authorized agent's printed name and signature. Forms are retained two years in the UMR. Overnight storage requires SECRET container or daily return to MPO. UMO initials each PS Form 3883 daily.

Service members must provide current directory file information to the UMR when checking in/out.

Transferred Personnel. All service members are required to check out with the UMR. Service members must provide a forwarding address or instructions on how to handle mail received after transfer. Failure to do so may result in the delay of forwarding or delivery of their mail.

0139.title: RECEPTACLES ISSUED TO AUTHORIZED PATRONS

quotes.text: Receptacles are assigned to personnel where USPS does not provide mail delivery service. Do not issue receptacles to military retirees who are not authorized MPO privileges or reside in the U.S.

Exceptions to policy may be granted via a written request to the MACOM. Request must contain duration and special circumstance that serves the best interest of the command. Receptacles are not authorized to be used for any private business venture.
An advance receptacle may be reserved/issued under the Sponsorship Program. Do not assign the receptacle earlier than 90 days prior to the first day of the reporting month.

Do not reissue a receptacle to a former member unless proof of MPO privilege status is established. Receptacles must only be reissued to a member twice for failure to claim mail. If the sponsor fails to claim mail from the receptacle, close the receptacle, and provide general delivery service.

regulatory.summary: Receptacles are issued to authorized patrons only where USPS does not provide service; not to retirees without MPO privileges or residing in the U.S.; not for private business. 0140.title: RECEPTACLE ASSIGNMENT FORMS AND INFORMATION

quotes.text: Record the member's name, date of receptacle issue, receptacle combination, and the combination change date on a DD Form 2262, Receptacle Record (Figure 4-1). Record the member's name, rank, EDIPI and receptacle assignment date on the DFC. List the sponsored dependent names in DROPPED block, and annotate receptacle number in UNIT ASSIGNED block. File the DFC per the DFC system requirements.

Provide the combination or key to the member on DD Form 2263, Mailing Address/Combination Notice (Figure 4-2), or similar notice. Advise the sponsor to memorize the combination and to read the general instructions on the form. The combination must not be given to anyone other than the member.

0142.title: RECEPTACLE KEY LOCK CYLINDER/COMBINATION PROCEDURES

quotes.text: Key/Combination Maintenance. a. Change the key lock cylinder or combination when believed to be compromised and when a receptacle is closed. b. Closed receptacles shall be changed within 5 working days. c. Record the member's name, receptacle issue date, and serial number of the key or the combination on the DD Form 2262. d. Ensure an adequate supply of key lock cylinders are retained. Cylinders locks may be rotated with other separate nests of receptacles. Rotate cylinder locks from each nest every 3 months. e. Receptacles must be maintained without charge to the customer. Retain at least two duplicate keys for each receptacle. The combination entered on the DD Form 2262 is the only combination retained. Keep duplicate keys and combinations in a safe place out of the customer's reach.

regulatory.summary: Cylinders or combinations must be changed on suspected compromise and on receptacle closure (within 5 working days). DD Form 2262 records member name, issue date, and key serial or combination. Cylinder locks are rotated every 3 months. At least two duplicate keys retained, stored out of customer reach.

0143.title: RECEPTACLE MAINTENANCE AND RECORDS

quotes.text: Receptacle Maintenance and Records. a. Coordinate receptacle numbering scheme with postal activity to maintain USPS addressing standards and avoid number duplication. b. Do not make major repairs to receptacles. Facility Engineers are responsible for repairs. c. Lubricate combination or key lock wheels with a small amount of powdered or flaked graphite on the moving parts of the lock. d. Check the receptacle windows and doors frequently for damage. e. Receptacles that cannot be secured/repaired shall be closed, and a new receptacle must be issued to the service member. f. Maintain a separate DD Form 2262 for each receptacle. Keep separate files for active, unassigned, and inactive forms. (1) File forms for assigned receptacles numerically in active file. (2) File forms for unassigned receptacles pending final process numerically in a file marked, Do Not Issue. (3) Maintain an inactive file by the latest date closure for unassigned receptacles.

regulatory.summary: Receptacle numbering coordinated with postal activity. Major repairs by Facility Engineers. Lubrication with graphite. Frequent inspection. Unrepairable receptacles closed and replaced. DD Form 2262 maintained per receptacle in active, unassigned (Do Not Issue), and inactive files.

actions: Confirm inactive file by latest date closure.

actions: Inspect windows and doors for damage.

(2) Mail addressed to Commander of a receptacle number that does not contain an addressee name or unit designation, must be endorsed with INSUFFICIENT ADDRESS and returned to sender. (3) Do not deliver mail addressed as occupant or resident of a receptacle number. Endorse it with INSUFFICIENT ADDRESS and return to sender.

0147.title: DD FORM 2258 TEMPORARY MAIL DISPOSITION

quotes.text: Prepare a DD Form 2258 when service member status is received from other than the member e.g., 1stSgt, section OIC, Unit Mail Clerk, unit diary, morning report, etc. Provide point of contact/telephone number in the special instructions block (Figure 4-4). Hold the mail until the member provides disposition instructions. Annotate the DFC and process the mail according to the information received.

Use DD Form 2258 for marking receptacles of members who are absent temporarily. The member's signature authorizes appropriate mail disposition. Members may provide a written designation for other authorized member to receipt for their mail during a temporary absence. (1) DD Form 2258 is not authorized for more than 90 days from the effective date. An absence of more than 90 days shall require a receptacle closure. Customers must provide a forwarding address for a 90 day or more absence. Assign a new receptacle to customer on return. (2) There is no limit to the number of times a customer may use a DD Form 2258. However, using consecutive DD Forms 2258 for the purpose of holding mail in a receptacle for longer than 90 consecutive days is not authorized.
Members in a confined, UA, or alleged deceased status shall be annotated on DD Form 2258. Update member status as required or until receptacle is closed.

regulatory.summary: DD Form 2258 captures temporary mail disposition instructions for absent members, signed by the member. Maximum effect is 90 days from date; longer absence requires closure. Consecutive use to extend hold past 90 days is prohibited. Confined, UA, and alleged deceased status also annotated on DD Form 2258.

0148.title: GENERAL DELIVERY SERVICE FOR DUE-IN MEMBERS

quotes.text: Personnel Due to Arrive (1) Endorse the mail with the arrival date and hold mail for 15 days past the anticipated arrival date. (2) If there is no arrival date, but there is an official indication of arrival, hold the mail for 30 days. (3) Screen hold mail weekly against the directory and annotate appropriately.

General Delivery Service. a. Provide general delivery service for members not expected to reside in barracks or quarters until a permanent quarters address is established. b. General delivery service may not exceed 90 days after the residence is established. c. General delivery mail must be delivered within 15 days of MPO receipt. Undelivered mail must be provided directory service i.e., personnel due to arrive, etc.

regulatory.summary: General delivery service applies to members not expected to reside in barracks or quarters until permanent quarters established (max 90 days after residence established). General delivery mail must be delivered within 15 days of MPO receipt. Due-to-arrive mail is held 15 days past arrival date or 30 days when only an official arrival indication exists, with weekly directory screening.

0149.title: NAVMC 10572 DIRECTORY FILE CARDS

quotes.text: All personnel attached to the command must complete a NAVMC 10572, Directory File Card (DFC).

The DFC information can be extracted from Marine Corps Total Force Structure records to maintain an accurate DFC system. DFCs shall be utilized as follows: (1) When a service member checks in, enter the following information on the DFC; last name, first name, middle initial, grade, Electronic Data Interchange Personal Identifier (EDIPI), unit joined from address, date, and assigned unit/section (Figure 3-9). DFCs must be filed alphabetically.

Retention Periods (1) UMRs must retain the DFC/COAs for 12 months after detachment of all permanently assigned service members. The discard date is the 13 month of departure. (2) DFCs for service members in a temporary status of six months or less shall be maintained for six months after the service member's departure. (3) The Unit Mail Clerk must purge the DFC/COA during the first week of each month. Destroy cards containing a discard date for that month.

regulatory.summary: All personnel must complete a NAVMC 10572 DFC. DFCs are filed alphabetically with name, grade, EDIPI, joined-from address, date, and assigned unit/section. Retention is 12 months after detachment of permanently assigned members and 6 months for temporary status (six months or less). Purge in first week of each month.

0150.title: PROCESSING DIRECTORY MAIL

quotes.text: Undeliverable-As-Addressed Mail. UMRs shall provide directory service to all UAA mail. Each piece of mail must be endorsed by the UMR with the reason (Figure 3-12). Absentee balloting materials must be tied or placed on top of letter bundles or in the front face of tray. Processed UAA must be returned to the MPO for further disposition.

Transferred Personnel. All service members are required to check out with the UMR. Service members must provide a forwarding address or instructions on how to handle mail received after transfer. (1) Readdress mail for transferred personnel by drawing a diagonal line through the incorrect portion of the address, and place the correct address to the right of the original address. (2) Block barcode information on front/back of mail piece. (3) The Unit Mail Clerk shall place on the backside of the mail piece the endorsement FORWARDED or FWD along with the date, unit, and the Unit Mail Clerk's initials. (4) Example - FWD/DATE/UNIT/UNIT MAIL CLERK'S INITIALS

No Record. Mail received with no record available must be returned to sender. (1) The Unit Mail Clerk shall draw a diagonal line through the incorrect portion of the address. (2) Block barcode information on front/back of mail piece. (3) The Unit Mail Clerk shall place on the backside of the mail piece the endorsement NO RECORD or NR along with the date, unit, and the Unit Mail Clerk's initials. (4) Example - NR/DATE/UNIT/UNIT MAIL CLERK'S INITIALS

Missent Mail. Mail inadvertently received for another unit is considered missent mail. (1) The Unit Mail Clerk must not make any markings to the front of the mail. (2) The Unit Mail Clerk shall place on the backside of the mail piece the endorsement MISSENT or MS along with the date, unit, and the Unit Mail Clerk's initials. (3) Example - MS/DATE/UNIT/UNIT MAIL CLERK'S INITIALS

regulatory.summary: Directory mail processing covers UAA (endorsed with reason and returned to MPO), Transferred (FWD endorsement), No Record (NR endorsement to sender), and Missent (MS endorsement). Each endorsement is on the back of the mail piece with date, unit, and Mail Clerk initials. Barcodes are blocked on transferred and No Record mail.

The OMP is supported by appropriated funds. The OMP provides effective policies/procedures in cost management, mail design, mail class, special services, address standards, etc. Activities and organizations supported by the OMP must comply with this Order.

Official mail is used solely for the business of the U.S. Government. Misuse of official mail may result in criminal charges. Authorized entities are responsible to determine eligibility of matter for official mailing, per references (a) and (c).

0203.title: COMMAND OFFICIAL MAIL CENTER ESTABLISHED

quotes.text: Command OMCs are required for units that operate a UMR and tenant activities that process official mail.
The UMR must not serve as the Command OMC. Unit Mail Officers and Mail Clerks may be assigned collateral official mail duties.
Establish a Command OMC near the Commander, headquarters staff, or UMR. Commanders are responsible for the security of the Command OMC.

0204.title: OMM AND ASSISTANT OMM APPOINTMENT AT E-6/GS-6 OR ABOVE

quotes.text: Any activity/organization with an official mail function must appoint in writing an E6/GS-6 or above to serve as OMMs (Figure 5-1). Assistant OMMs must be appointed to perform the OMM duties when the OMM is absent. The appointments may be assigned as an additional duty.
A written request may be sent to the MACOM for wavier to grade requirements. An approved exception to policy must be annotated on the appointment letter.

Distribute appointment letters as follows: (1) Units and staff element OMMs send copies to Installation OMM. (2) Tenant unit OMMs send copies to host Installation OMM. (3) Installation OMMs send copies to HQMC MFP-3 OMM. (4) Remote OMCs that operate an official postage metering site or a web mailing platform shall send copies to HQMC MFP-3 OMM.

regulatory.summary: OMM and Assistant OMM must be appointed in writing at E-6/GS-6 or above. Grade waivers require written MACOM request with the exception annotated on the appointment letter. Copies must be distributed per Ch. 5 par 3c.

howToInspect.callout: OMM or Assistant OMM not appointed in writing

actions: Confirm appointee at E-6/GS-6 or above, or pull MACOM waiver.
howToInspect.callout: Appointee below E-6/GS-6 without MACOM waiver

actions: Confirm copies sent to Installation OMM (or HQMC MFP-3 OMM as applicable).

0205.title: OMM AND ASSISTANT OMM TRAINING

quotes.text: OMMs must receive training from the Installation OMM within 90 days utilizing the HQMC MFP-3 Mail Clerk Indoctrination Course. Mail handling responsibilities and training objectives shall be reinforced by routine distribution. OMMs must receive annual updates from the Installation OMM.

The Mail Clerk Indoctrination Course certificate must be filed at the MPO/unit for two years. Transferred personnel may present the certificate to meet subsequent official mail assignments at gaining command/unit.

regulatory.summary: OMMs and Assistant OMMs must complete the Mail Clerk Indoctrination Course training from the Installation OMM within 90 days of appointment, receive annual updates, and file the certificate at the unit for two years.

No annual OMM training updates

0206.title: PS FORM 8139 SIGNED BY UNIT MAIL OFFICERS AND CLERKS

quotes.text: Ensure the Unit Mail Officer and Assistant Unit Mail Officer(s) sign the PS Form 8139 - Your Role in Protecting the Security of the United States Mail.
Unit Mail Clerks must be informed and acknowledge their responsibilities in handling mail via a signed PS Form 8139 - Your Role in Protecting the Security of the United States Mail (Figure 3-2).

regulatory.summary: Unit Mail Officers, Assistants, and Unit Mail Clerks must each sign PS Form 8139 acknowledging mail handling responsibilities.

0207.title: UNIT MAIL CLERKS APPOINTED FOR UNITS WITHOUT UMR

quotes.text: Activities that do not maintain a UMR are required to appoint Unit Mail Clerks to deliver official mail to authorized agents.

A Unit Mail Officer and Assistant Unit Mail Officer must be assigned to supervise the primary operation of each UMR. Additional Assistant Unit Mail Officers can be assigned to organic detachments, deployed elements, etc.

0208.title: AUTHORIZATION TO RECEIVE AND OPEN OFFICIAL MAIL (REPEAT OF Q0104 FOR OMC SCOPE)

quotes.text: Commanding Officers must designate in writing personnel e.g., Unit OMM, etc., authorized to receipt and open official mail, to include official accountable mail addressed to the Commanding Officer. Official mail/official accountable mail must not be opened in the UMR under any circumstance.
Unit Mail Clerks/Orderlies shall not be authorized agents on the designation letter to receipt and open official mail/official accountable mail.

The authorization letter must be signed by the current Commanding Officer. By Direction authority is not authorized. The authorization letter must contain a sample signature of all individuals authorized.

Separate letters may authorize more than one individual for each type of mail. However, any changes to an authorization letter shall void the authority and require a new authorization letter. Authorization letters must be maintained for two years after cancellation in the UMR files.

regulatory.summary: The current Commanding Officer must designate, in writing, personnel authorized to receive and open official and official accountable mail. Letters must be signed by the current CO (no By Direction authority), include sample signatures, exclude Unit Mail Clerks/Orderlies, and be retained two years after cancellation. This requirement applies in the OMC context as well. Inspector Note: Q0208 duplicates Q0104 with focus on the OMC. Confirm the same authorization letter covers both UMR receipt and OMC receipt or that a separate OMC letter exists.

0209.title: CHAIN OF RECEIPTS FOR OFFICIAL ACCOUNTABLE MAIL (OMC SCOPE)

quotes.text: A chain of delivery receipts shall cover all accountable mail. The Unit Mail Clerk must be able to account for all accountable mail. Accountability shall be provided by physical possession of article, or by showing delivery of article to authorized agent.

The serving post office shall prepare a PS Form 3883, Firm Delivery Receipt for Accountable and Bulk Delivery Mail (Figure 3-8). The MPO or serving post office must retain the original copy. The Unit Mail Clerk shall receive a copy with the mail piece. The Unit Mail Clerk shall ensure the accountable mail pieces are listed on PS Form 3883 and are in good delivery condition. PS Forms 3883 must be retained in the UMR for two years.

regulatory.summary: The same chain-of-receipts requirement applies in the OMC. PS Form 3883 covers all accountable mail with chain of receipts maintained two years.

0210.title: OMM VERIFIES DELIVERY RECORDS PROPERLY

quotes.text: The Unit Mail Officer must initial daily each PS Form 3883 for effective delivery verification. A transfer of accountable mail to subsequent Unit Mail Clerk must require delivery on PS Form 3883. A new PS Form 3883 shall be prepared to make effective delivery to an authorized agent.

regulatory.summary: The OMM (analogous to the UMO in the UMR context) must initial each PS Form 3883 daily for delivery verification.

howToInspect.callout: OMM not initialing PS Form 3883 daily

0211.title: OFFICIAL MAIL ADDRESS VERIFICATION AND USE

quotes.text: Official Mail Addresses. HQMC MFP-3 maintains the current list of authorized Marine Corps official mail addresses. Official mail addresses are updated annually. Official mail addresses are available at the HQMC MFP-3 website.

Address Standards. To ensure efficient mail delivery a current/correct formatted address must be used. The following addressing standards are required on all official mail: a. Use complete official mailing address on both the return address and the delivery address (Figure 5-3). b. Addresses must be print/machine typed. Return/delivery addresses shall be in black ink using upper case letters. Address characters must not touch and equally spaced. The print size for address characters is 10 to 12 points. c. Use no punctuation except for the hyphen in the ZIP+4 code. d. Use the correct address format. Delivery/return official addresses are limited to five lines and formatted with uniform left margin. The attention line is optional and can be inserted within the second line of the address.

regulatory.summary: Official mail addresses must be verified against the current HQMC MFP-3 list and applied per Ch. 5 par 19 address standards (uppercase, black ink, no punctuation except ZIP+4 hyphen, five lines, uniform left margin, 10-12 pt).

actions: Pull a sample of outgoing pieces.

howToInspect.callout: Addresses not validated against HQMC MFP-3 list

actions: Confirm address matches current authorized list.

questionsToAsk: Where do you verify current official mail addresses?
verification: Addresses validated against HQMC MFP-3 list.

0212.title: CURRENT OMC PUBLICATIONS AND DIRECTIVES

quotes.text: Publications. The following is a listing of required publications for cost effective use of the OMP. Hard copies of these publications are required only when access to electronic copies are not available. The publications can be obtained via the Installation OMM. These publications are required at all OMC locations. Command OMCs without a postage meter are only required to maintain the below (i) and (j) publications: a. Domestic Mail Manual. b. International Mail Manual. c. Current Postal Bulletin. d. USPS Notice 123, Price List. e. Publication 25, Designing Letter and Reply Mail. f. Notice 67, Automation Letters Template. g. Publication 28, Postal Addressing Standards. h. Publication 542, Understanding the Private Express Statutes. i. DoDI 4525.09, Military Postal Service. j. MCO 5110.4B, Marine Corps Postal Affairs and Official Mail Program.

regulatory.summary: OMCs must maintain current publications a-j. OMCs without a postage meter are only required to maintain (i) DoDI 4525.09 and (j) MCO 5110.4B. Electronic copies satisfy the requirement when available.

For meter OMCs, confirm a-j present.
For non-meter OMCs, confirm i and j present.

0213.title: COMMAND OMC AS CONTROL POINT FOR OUTGOING OFFICIAL MATTER

quotes.text: Command OMCs must serve as a control point for outgoing official matter. Command OMCs shall collect, screen, consolidate, and approve matter for official mailing.

regulatory.summary: The Command OMC must serve as the control point for outgoing official matter, collecting, screening, consolidating, and approving items for official mailing.

howToInspect.callout: Outgoing official mail bypasses OMC control point

0214.title: EXTRA SERVICES PROVISIONS

quotes.text: Extra Services. The use of extra services is limited to law, DoD Instructions, or Marine Corps directives. Additional fees are charged for extra services. Extra services are authorized for Marine Corps use as follows: a. Priority Mail Express. Priority Mail Express is expedited mail service. Careful consideration should be taken to ensure expedited service is an essential requirement to mission accomplishment and not a matter of convenience. b. Registered Mail. Registered mail provides added mail security and evidence of mailing/delivery. Registered items must be sent as first-class or priority mail. c. Certified Mail. Certified mail provides evidence of mailing and a record of delivery. It may be used only for first-class or priority mail having no intrinsic value. e. Insured Mail. Insured mail may be used for proof of delivery of valuable administrative/logistical equipment, supplies, and printed matter.

regulatory.summary: Extra services use is limited to law, DoDI, or Marine Corps directives. OMMs must understand authorized uses for Priority Mail Express, Registered Mail, Certified Mail, and Insured Mail per Ch. 5 par 17 a-e. Inspector Note: MPM par 41.1.c is cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify the corresponding MPM paragraph on site as MPM updates separately.

actions: Ask the OMM to describe authorized uses of each extra service.
Pull a sample of mailings with extra services applied.
howToInspect.callout: Registered Mail used for non-priority mail (prohibited)

actions: Confirm extra service use matches authorized categories.

0215.title: MAXIMUM CONSOLIDATION OF CORRESPONDENCE

quotes.text: OMMs must ensure that official mailings are consolidated to reduce costs. Routine correspondence or documents shall be delivered to the OMC for consolidation prior to mailing.
Use the smallest possible envelope for a single consolidated mailing. Minimum mailing standards must be strictly followed to avoid surcharges.

Consolidated mailings must be addressed to the Commanding General/Officer of that activity. A bold mailing endorsement, CONTAINS CONSOLIDATED CORRESPONDENCE, shall be placed on address side and lower left quadrant of the consolidated container.
OMCs receiving mail endorsed CONTAINS CONSOLIDATED CORRESPONDENCE are authorized to open this mail and make appropriate distribution.

regulatory.summary: OMMs must consolidate routine correspondence at the OMC prior to mailing, use the smallest possible envelope, address to CG/CO of the receiving activity, and apply the CONTAINS CONSOLIDATED CORRESPONDENCE endorsement on address side and lower left quadrant.

Confirm endorsement on address side and lower left quadrant.

0301.title: POSTAGE METERS AND STAMPS SECURED

quotes.text: Access and operation of postage metering equipment is limited to authorized personnel. Remove the meter and place it in a locked safe/file cabinet or locked room when not in use.
Meters requiring a code or password for operation are not required to be removed during temporary operator absences.
Meters that lock in place on mailing machines are not required to be removed at night, as long as the mailing machine is in a secure area.

OMMs are responsible for preventing the theft, misuse, waste/loss of postage stamps, stamped envelopes, and postal cards. The postage stamp inventory shall be centralized and closely controlled by the activity OMM. Inventory must be secured in a locked room or locked container e.g., safe, file cabinet, desk drawer, etc.

regulatory.summary: Postage meters must be limited to authorized personnel and stored in a locked safe, cabinet, or locked room when not in use (unless password-protected or locked in place in a secure area). Postage stamp inventory must be centralized and secured in a locked container.

0302.title: POSTAGE METER KEYS AND COMBINATIONS SECURED

quotes.text: Keep extra keys and current meter combinations in a secure envelope. The meter operator and the OMM shall sign on the seal of the envelope to validate the security of the keys/combinations. The envelopes must be stored in a locked receptacle.

regulatory.summary: Extra meter keys and current combinations must be kept in a secure envelope signed on the seal by the meter operator and OMM and stored in a locked receptacle.

0303.title: POSTAGE STAMP INVENTORY MAINTAINED

quotes.text: A postage stamp inventory is intended for small volume mailers. Small volume mailer inventories shall not exceed a 90-day supply. Activities without a backup metering application may maintain no more than a 10-day emergency supply of postage stamps. Deployable units may maintain a 60-day supply of postage stamps for locations without meters.

quotes.text: Monthly audits are required for active postage stamp inventories. Quarterly audits are required for reserve postage stamp inventories.

regulatory.summary: Monthly audits are required for active postage stamp inventories. Quarterly audits are required for reserve postage stamp inventories. Inspector Note: DODI 4525.09 par 3.11.c is cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify the corresponding paragraph on site as DODI text may have updated since policy issuance.

0305.title: DAILY RECORD OF METER REGISTER

quotes.text: Daily Record of Meter Register is made for each postage meter placed into service. The postage meter accounts for total postage available and expended.
The supervisor shall record the date, ascending/descending register, and serial number. A daily record/register print out can be used for records accountability.
At the end of each business day, the meter operator must verify the ascending/descending register and total.

Supervisor verification of the register is required prior to the setting of additional funds. Funds added to meter machine must be accounted/verified via logbook or computer printout. Registry discrepancies after vendor setting shall be reported to vendor for corrective action.
Your official mail control site shall report these amounts when required. Logbook or computer printout must be retained on file for 2 years from date of final entry.

regulatory.summary: Each postage meter must have a daily register record showing date, ascending/descending register, and serial number, verified by operator end-of-day and by supervisor prior to fund setting. Records are retained two years. Inspector Note: MPM Appendix 25A, Table 5, Steps 1-2 is cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify the corresponding MPM appendix on site.

actions: Pull current month and prior 12 months.
Confirm date, ascending/descending register, and serial number captured.
howToInspect.callout: Daily meter register record missing required fields

howToInspect.callout: Records not retained two years

0306.title: PS FORM 3533 PROCESSING FOR REFUND OF POSTAGE

quotes.text: Per reference (q), credit the postage meter account for incorrect postage or spoiled tapes as follows: (1) Complete/submit a PS Form 3533, Application for Voucher for Refund of Postage and Fees, to the local USPS representative. (2) Attach voucher and unused meter stamps/tapes, ensuring that: (a) Stamps/tapes are complete and legible. (b) Stamps/tapes are affixed on envelope/wrapper bearing the name and address of the addressee. Attach the entire envelope as necessary. (c) Loose stamps/tapes are submitted separately. (3) The USPS representative shall complete the PS Form 3533. A copy of PS Form 3533 must be provided. Cash or checks must not be accepted from the USPS representative for the refund. A credit for amount shall be applied to meter account and invoice.
Spoiled meter tapes/stamps adjustments must be annotated in the postage expenditure report.

regulatory.summary: Incorrect postage or spoiled tapes require a PS Form 3533 submitted to USPS with the unused stamps/tapes. Credit is applied to the meter account; cash or check refunds are not authorized. Spoiled adjustments are annotated in the postage expenditure report. Inspector Note: USPS DMM 604 par 9.3 is cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify the corresponding USPS DMM section on site as USPS publications update independently.

actions: Confirm completed PS Form 3533 with attached stamps/tapes.
howToInspect.callout: PS Form 3533 incomplete

0307.title: WEEKLY ANALYSIS OF POSTAGE EXPENDED

quotes.text: A postage management record can provide standard or custom data e.g., postage expense, date, mail class, unit/section mail volume, etc., as required. Locations that do not have electronic accountability must manually record standard postage management information.

The record of postage expended shall be analyzed by the OMM weekly. Analysis shall be used to identify large volume mailers and routine mail class/special services used. Mailers shall be encouraged to use other economical means to improve the OMP.
The record is used for the annual Postage Expenditure Report.

regulatory.summary: The OMM must analyze the record of postage expended weekly to identify large-volume mailers and routine mail classes/special services and recommend economical alternatives. The record feeds the annual Postage Expenditure Report.

actions: Pull current period and prior 8 weeks.
howToInspect.callout: Weekly OMM postage analysis not conducted

actions: Confirm analysis identifies large-volume mailers and routine services.

questionsToAsk: When was your last weekly analysis?
What recommendations did you make?

0308.title: COST SAVING MEASURES APPLIED

quotes.text: Cost Management. OMMs shall use the following OMP cost saving measures. a. Monitor mail practices and ensure mail is sent by the most economical means. b. Provide training to personnel who originate or prepare mailings. c. Use standard letter-size envelopes whenever possible. Large envelopes shall be used only when material may be damaged by folding. d. Use eligible presorted or marketing mail discounts. e. Ensure items can be mailed and not subject to surcharges. f. Reduce mailing frequency and volume. g. Review necessary mailings. h. Update mailing lists annually or as changes become known. i. Consider other alternatives for mailing publications. j. Require adequate printing lead time for economical mail service. k. Reduce publication weight by printing on both sides of the paper and selecting the lightest paper weight. l. Ensure that contracts for printing and distribution contain DoD specifications. m. Promote electronic formats that make maximum use of available print space to reduce the weight of mailings. n. Consider alternatives to hard copy mailings, such as: (1) Telephone, email, or fax (2) Courier or Guard mail (3) Distribution Management Office (4) Commercial Carriers e.g., FEDEX, UPS, or DHL (5) Naval Message.
OMMs must ensure that official mailings are consolidated to reduce costs. Routine correspondence or documents shall be delivered to the OMC for consolidation prior to mailing.

regulatory.summary: OMMs must apply Ch. 5 par 20 cost-saving measures (a through n) and ensure consolidation per par 22b.

actions: Confirm OMM applies the cost-saving measures.
howToInspect.callout: No cost-saving measures evidenced

actions: Confirm mailing-list updates, training, consolidation practice.
Letter-size envelopes not used as default.

questionsToAsk: Which cost-saving measures do you actively apply?

0309.title: PRIORITY MAIL EXPRESS PROVISIONS

quotes.text: Priority Mail Express. Priority Mail Express is expedited mail service. Careful consideration should be taken to ensure expedited service is an essential requirement to mission accomplishment and not a matter of convenience. (1) CONUS Priority Mail Express service provides a 1-day or 2-day delivery guarantee. OCONUS Priority Mail Express service is only available to the International Service Center gateways. (2) The following questions should be answered prior to using Priority Mail Express: (a) What mission failure would result if document is not received the following day? (b) What valid circumstance prevents the item from being ready in time for normal mail transit? (c) Is a recipient available to accept document? (d) Have other less expensive or alternatives been considered?

regulatory.summary: Priority Mail Express is limited to essential mission requirements (not convenience). The four pre-use questions must be answered before use. CONUS provides 1-2 day guarantee; OCONUS limited to International Service Center gateways. Inspector Note: MPM par 41.1.c, e, g is cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify the corresponding MPM paragraphs on site.

howToInspect.step: Pull Recent Priority Mail Express Use

howToInspect.callout: Priority Mail Express used without documented justification

questionsToAsk: When did you last use Priority Mail Express?
What was the justification?

0310.title: CLASSIFICATION OF OFFICIAL ITEMS CORRECTLY ENDORSED

quotes.text: Selecting Mail Class. The USPS divides mail by classification. Each class of mail has different features, service levels, postage prices, and presort requirements. The classification of official mail is determined by the type of item being sent, the weight of the item, and extra service requirements. a. First Class Mail. First-Class mail are items weighing less than 13 ounces. b. Priority Mail. (1) Priority mail are items weighing 13 ounces to 70 pound max weight. Maximum combined length and girth is 108 inches. Priority mail is mailed at the First Class rate. Endorse each mail piece, Priority Mail. c. Periodicals. d. Marketing Mail. e. Retail Ground. Retail Ground are items not required to be mailed as First-Class Mail. Maximum weight is 70 pounds and may not exceed 130 inches length and girth. Pieces must be endorsed, Retail Ground. f. Package Services. (1) Bound Printed Matter (BPM). (2) Media Mail. Mailings must be endorsed, Media Mail. (3) Library Mail.

regulatory.summary: Mail class is determined by type, weight, and extra service requirements. Priority Mail, Retail Ground, and Media Mail each require a specific endorsement on the piece.

howToInspect.callout: Priority Mail/Retail Ground/Media Mail endorsement missing or wrong class

actions: Confirm Priority Mail, Retail Ground, Media Mail endorsements where applicable.
Wrong class selected for weight or content.

questionsToAsk: How do you select mail class?
verification: Endorsements present for Priority Mail, Retail Ground, Media Mail.
Class selection matches weight and content.

0311.title: APPROPRIATED FUNDS FOR POSTAGE PROPERLY USED

quotes.text: The OMP is supported by appropriated funds. The OMP provides effective policies/procedures in cost management, mail design, mail class, special services, address standards, etc. Activities and organizations supported by the OMP must comply with this Order.

Official mail is used solely for the business of the U.S. Government. Misuse of official mail may result in criminal charges. Authorized entities are responsible to determine eligibility of matter for official mailing, per references (a) and (c).

Official mail shall not be used for the following: (1) For private use e.g., holiday/birthday cards, unofficial retirement announcements, job resumes, any social function, etc. (2) Non-appropriated Fund Instrumentalities (NAFI) or their functions. (3) By retired service members, unless acting in an official capacity.

regulatory.summary: OMP appropriated funds are limited solely to U.S. Government business. Misuse may result in criminal charges. Prohibited uses include private correspondence, NAFI functions, retired service members not acting officially, and other unauthorized categories per Ch. 5 par 12c. Inspector Note: MPM par 41.1.a-b and 41.2.ff is cited in the checklist. Inspector should verify the corresponding MPM paragraphs on site.

actions: Pull samples and assess legitimacy of official use.
howToInspect.callout: Official postage used for private/NAFI/retired-member purpose (prohibited)

actions: Confirm OMM reports misuse to Installation OMM.
Private correspondence sent on official postage.

questionsToAsk: What is your process when you suspect official mail misuse?
verification: Sample outgoing mail demonstrates official use.

0312.title: POSTAGE EXPENDITURE REPORTS SUBMITTED

quotes.text: Per reference (r), HQMC MFP-3 must submit an annual OMM/GSA mail management report to the DoD OMM utilizing SMART via AMPS (Figure 5-4).

Installations and activities authorized to use web mailing platforms/meters or prepaid postage e.g., permit, BRM, etc., must submit expenditure reports as follows: (1) Access to AMPS is authorized via the HQMC (MRP-3) OMM. (2) Reports are submitted each fiscal year. (3) The deadline to submit reports is no later than 7 days after the reporting period ends.

Each location shall report the following data, as applicable: (1) USPS Single Piece Mailing by type of mail class with expenditures and number of pieces. (2) USPS Permit Imprint by type of service with expenditures and number of pieces. (3) Contract fees, vendor fees, etc. (4) Comments section: This report covers the FY__.

0313.title: REGIONAL OMP INSPECTIONS EACH FISCAL YEAR

quotes.text: Official Mail Program Inspections. Installation or equivalent OMMs must inspect staff activities, subordinate activities, and tenant activities each fiscal year, per references (c) and (r). These inspection reports must be filed for two years and made available for review.
